    ],

        "africa", "middle east", "latin america", "kabul", "baghdad"
    ]
}

# Pre-compile regex
patterns = {}
for cat, keywords in categories.items():
    # Escape keywords just in case, though they are mostly alphanumeric
    # Join with |
    pattern_str = r'\b(' + '|'.join(map(re.escape, keywords)) + r')\b'
    patterns[cat] = re.compile(pattern_str, re.IGNORECASE)

def classify_article_regex(title, description):
    text = (title + " " + description) # Regex handles case
    scores = {cat: 0 for cat in categories}
    
    for cat, pattern in patterns.items():
        matches = pattern.findall(text)
        scores[cat] = len(matches)
        
    # Logic refinements
    if "video game" in text.lower() or "gameboy" in text.lower():
        scores["Science/Technology"] += 5
        
    if "oil" in text.lower() and "price" in text.lower():
        scores["Business"] += 2
        
    best_cat = max(scores, key=scores.get)
    if max(scores.values()) == 0:
        return "World" # Default
    return best_cat

}

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps(result))

strong_tech = ["science", "laboratory", "research"]
